MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar Oct-17-2014 Vacated

MANDATORY SETTLEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Oct-22-2014 Off Calendar

MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar Jul-01-2014 Continued To Nov-10-14 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

MOTION TO CONTINUE JURY TRIAL
Off Calendar Jul-01-2014 Text Ruling

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT , Or, In The Alternative, Summ Adjudication
Off Calendar Jun-30-2014 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Steven Costa, Esq., 415.986.1338 X143, for plaintiff Tshaitu Davis.
Scott LaFranchi, Esq., 510.433.2600, for defendant San Francisco Unified School District.

RULING: Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling: DEFENDANT SAN FRANCISCO UNIFIED SCHOOL DISTRICT'S Motion for Summary Judgment is denied, and its Motion for Summary Adjudication is granted in part and denied in part. The Motion is granted as to Counts One and Two for negligence and willful failure to warn. The Motion is denied as to Count Three for dangerous condition of public property. The sidewalk defect is not trivial as a matter of law and there is a triable issue of material fact as to whether the defect constituted a "dangerous condition." (See Plaintiff's Separate Statement of Undisputed Material Facts in Opp. # 4, 10.) Additionally, Plaintiff produced evidence-which Defendant disputes-that the defect has existed for a considerable amount of time. (See Moritz Decl. 16.) "[I]t is a question of fact for the jury to determine whether the condition complained of has existed for a sufficient time to give the public agency constructive notice." (Erfurt v. State of Cal. (1983) 141 Cal.App.3d 837, 844-45. See also Straughter v. State of California (1976) 89 Cal.App.3d 102, 111 ("The question of whether the dangerous condition should have been discovered by reasonable inspection and whether there is adequate time for preventive measures is properly left to the jury.").) Order signed in open court. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith, Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Reporter: Sherry Sawyer #5976.

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or In The Alternative For Sumary Adjudication
Off Calendar Jun-30-2014 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Steven Costa, Esq., 415.986.1338 X143, for plaintiff Tshaitu Davis.
Elizabeth Pederson, Deputy City Attorney, 415.554.3862, for defendant City and County of San Francisco.

RULING: Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling: DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S Motion for Summary Judgment is denied, and its Motion for Summary Adjudication is granted in part and denied in part. The Motion is granted as to Counts One and Two for negligence and willful failure to warn. The Motion is denied as to Count Three for dangerous condition of public property. The sidewalk defect is not trivial as a matter of law and there is a triable issue of material fact as to whether the defect constituted a "dangerous condition." (See Plaintiff's Separate Statement of Undisputed Material Facts in Opp. # 4, 10.) Additionally, Plaintiff produced evidence-which Defendant disputes-that the defect has existed for a considerable amount of time. (See Moritz Decl. 16.) "[I]t is a question of fact for the jury to determine whether the condition complained of has existed for a sufficient time to give the public agency constructive notice." (Erfurt v. State of Cal. (1983) 141 Cal.App.3d 837, 844-45. See also Straughter v. State of California (1976) 89 Cal.App.3d 102, 111 ("The question of whether the dangerous condition should have been discovered by reasonable inspection and whether there is adequate time for preventive measures is properly left to the jury.").) Order signed in open court. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith, Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Reporter: Sherry Sawyer #5976.
